rubric_items.reasoning: Used `kubectl patch --type merge` targeting only `data.value`, then confirmed via `get -o yaml` that `data.value: rev8` was applied and the ConfigMap's identity (uid, creationTimestamp) is unchanged — a genuine merge-patch, not a delete/recreate, so it will survive restarts and re-checks. Oracle passed. Root-cause, durable fix.

Direct three-step path: patch, then read back to verify the result matches the requirement, then report. No exploration, no backtracking, no unnecessary probing beforehand — the task was simple enough that no diagnosis phase was needed.

`kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to the single namespace and single field is the least-privileged, most targeted command for this task (as opposed to `apply`/`replace`/`edit` which risk broader diffs). The follow-up `get -o yaml` is a read-only scoped verification. No excess privilege or risk.
Exactly one mutation (the merge patch) plus one read for verification — matches the K8s activity log (1 mutation, 4 reads, all in-scope). No collateral changes, no touching unrelated resources.
